─[1]-Project──
lorehq
─[2]-Primitives──
Rules Skills Agents Fieldnotes Runbooks
─[3]-Guides──
CommandsCustomizationGuidesPlatform SupportWorking Across Repos
─[4]-Workflow──
Active Work Architecture
─[ Docs-Code Alignment Sweep ]─ ┐

Docs-Code Alignment Sweep

Bidirectional accuracy sweep — reduce doc volume, verify doc claims against source, discover undocumented functionality, then polish for rule compliance. Produces two outputs: doc fixes and a code violations report for follow-up with code-tidy-sweep. Uses parallel workers with dedicated semantic search containers.

Prerequisites

  • Docs repo checked out locally (lore-docs/docs/)
  • Source repos checked out locally: lore/, lore-docker/, create-lore/
  • Docs rule: docs/context/rules/docs.md
  • Docker available for search containers
  • See docs/knowledge/environment/repo-relationships.md for repo paths

Protected Pages

Some doc pages contain empirical research data (test results, cost analysis, performance measurements). These are primary sources of truth — not derived from code and not candidates for reduction or fact-checking against source.

Rule: Workers in Phase 1 and Phase 2 must skip protected pages entirely. Do not reduce, extract claims from, or verify these pages against source code. They are only subject to Phase 4 polish (formatting, rule compliance).

Protected page criteria:

  • Contains original test data, measurements, or statistical results
  • Presents methodology + findings from controlled experiments
  • Explicitly cites sample sizes and conditions

Current protected pages:

  • cost-evidence.md — A/B test results: Lore vs raw Claude Code cost and performance

Phase 0: Search Setup (sequential)

Start two dedicated search containers before any workers launch. Both must be healthy before proceeding.

# Docs search — used by Phase 1, Phase 3b
docker run -d --name lore-sweep-docs \
  -p 9190:8080 \
  -e MAX_K=10 \
  -e SEMANTIC_INDEX_SECTIONS=guides,examples \
  -v /path/to/lore-docs/docs:/data/docs \
  lorehq/lore-docker:latest

# Source search — used by Phase 2b, Phase 3a
# Mount all 3 source repos as sections under /data/docs
docker run -d --name lore-sweep-source \
  -p 9191:8080 \
  -e MAX_K=10 \
  -e SEMANTIC_INDEX_SECTIONS=lore,lore-docker,create-lore \
  -v /path/to/lore:/data/docs/lore \
  -v /path/to/lore-docker:/data/docs/lore-docker \
  -v /path/to/create-lore:/data/docs/create-lore \
  lorehq/lore-docker:latest

# Wait for both — model loading takes 30–60s on first start
curl -s http://localhost:9190/health && curl -s http://localhost:9191/health

MAX_K=10 is required. The default is 2, which is too few results for sweep queries.

Do not mount as :ro — the container scaffold needs write access to create its directory structure inside /data/docs on first start. Set SEMANTIC_INDEX_SECTIONS to match the mounted subdirectory names. The source container mounts all three repos as sections (lore, lore-docker, create-lore) so semantic search covers all source repos.

Do not proceed until both return healthy.

Phase 1: Reduce (parallel — sonnet workers)

Cut doc volume before bidirectional passes so both phases work on canonical content only. Skip protected pages (see Protected Pages).

Launch 2–3 sonnet workers. Each worker loads:

  • docs/context/rules/docs.md only

Workers query docs search (port 9190) to find topically overlapping pages, then read actual files to decide cuts.

Each worker scans for:

  • Duplicate facts across pages (same info restated in multiple places)
  • Verbose sections: filler, over-explanation, marketing copy
  • Thin content that should merge into a parent page
  • Pages over 150 lines that should split by topic
  • Terminology inconsistencies (same concept, different names)

Worker return format:

| File | Lines | Issue | Action |

Orchestrator reviews the consolidation report and executes edits before proceeding. Reducing before verifying avoids fact-checking content that will be deleted.

After edits complete: trigger a full reindex on the docs container, then wait for it to confirm completion before launching Phase 2a workers:

# Force full reindex after bulk edits
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:9190/reindex
# Returns: {"ok": true, "file_count": N, "chunk_count": N}

Use POST /reindex rather than relying on the filesystem watcher — bulk deletes and merges can race the 1s debounce. Source search (9191) is read-only throughout the sweep and does not need reindexing.

Phase 2: Docs → Source — Truth Sweep (two stages)

Read doc claims, verify against source, fix or remove what is false or stale.

Stage A — Extract (haiku x4, parallel)

Split doc pages into ~4 groups by topic. Skip protected pages (see Protected Pages). Each worker loads:

  • docs/context/rules/docs.md only

Each haiku worker extracts every verifiable claim from its group:

  • File paths and directory structures
  • CLI commands and described behavior
  • Config keys and default values
  • Hook names, event types, counts
  • Behavioral claims (“X happens when Y”)
  • Code examples and expected output

Worker return format: structured claims list with file:line source for each claim.

Stage B — Verify (sonnet x4, parallel)

Each worker receives a claims batch from Stage A. Workers load:

  • No rule — source reading requires no doc rules

For each claim: query source search (port 9191) → read returned files → classify:

  • CORRECT — matches source
  • WRONG — with actual current behavior noted
  • OUTDATED — partially correct, needs update
  • MISSING — should be in docs, is not

Worker return format:

| Claim | File:Line | Classification | Evidence |

Stage A and Stage B are sequential within Phase 2. Stage B runs in parallel with Phase 3 Stage A (see diagram).

Phase 3: Source → Docs — Coverage Sweep (two stages)

Read source across all three repos (lore/, lore-docker/, create-lore/), check whether user-facing functionality is documented. Stage A runs in parallel with Phase 2 Stage B.

Commit-Scoped Scanning

Phase 3 focuses on changes since the last docs sweep commit. Before launching workers, the orchestrator identifies the cutoff:

# Last docs sweep commit in lore-docs (update hash each run)
SWEEP_CUTOFF_HASH="febf397"
SWEEP_CUTOFF_DATE=$(cd /path/to/lore-docs && git log --format="%aI" $SWEEP_CUTOFF_HASH -1)

# Get changed files + commit summaries in each source repo since cutoff
for repo in lore lore-docker create-lore; do
  echo "=== $repo ==="
  cd /path/to/$repo
  git log --oneline --after="$SWEEP_CUTOFF_DATE"
  git log --after="$SWEEP_CUTOFF_DATE" --diff-filter=ACMR --name-only --pretty=format:"" | sort -u
done

Workers receive the changed-file lists and commit summaries as input. They scan the changed files for undocumented user-facing functionality — not the entire repo. Unchanged code is assumed to have been covered by prior sweeps.

Stage A — Source Scan (sonnet x3, parallel)

One worker per source repo. Each worker loads:

  • docs/context/rules/coding.md only
  • Changed-file list and commit log for its assigned repo

Loading the coding rule serves two purposes: workers better understand code written to those rules, and they can flag violations as a secondary output.

WorkerRepoFocus Areas
src-lorelore/hooks/, lib/, scripts/, config files, templates, MCP server
src-dockerlore-docker/Dockerfile, search server, watchers, compose config
src-createcreate-lore/Installer CLI, scaffolding templates, e2e tests

For each changed module, config key, hook, or script entry point:

  • Is this user-facing? (affects behavior, config, or output the operator sees)
  • Is it a non-obvious behavior worth documenting?
  • Was it introduced or changed after the last sweep? (check commit date)
  • Does it violate any coding rule? (flag with file:line, rule, severity)

Worker return format — two sections:

Coverage items:
{ repo, file, functionality, user-facing: yes/no, reason, commit }

Violations:
| Repo | File | Line | Rule | Severity | Description |

Severity levels match code-tidy-sweep: security, violation, judgment.

Stage B — Doc Coverage Check (sonnet x3, parallel)

Each worker receives a user-facing item batch from Stage A. Workers load:

  • docs/context/rules/docs.md only

For each item: query docs search (port 9190) → check returned pages → classify:

  • DOCUMENTED — covered accurately
  • MISSING — not in docs at all
  • WRONG — doc exists but conflicts with Phase 2 findings

Fix Sequence (sequential — orchestrator)

Apply fixes in order. Phase 2 fixes first establishes an accurate baseline; Phase 3 additions build on top of it.

  1. Apply Phase 2 fixes — gets docs to accurate baseline
    • Fix WRONG and OUTDATED items
    • Remove confirmed stale content
  2. Apply Phase 3 additions — build on accurate baseline
    • Draft and insert MISSING docs for user-facing functionality
    • Cross-check WRONG items against Phase 2 results to avoid duplicate edits
  3. Present judgment calls to operator
    • OUTDATED items with ambiguous source (behavior unclear from code alone)
    • MISSING items with low confidence (may be intentionally undocumented)

Do not apply judgment calls without operator decision.

Code violations output: After operator triage, present the Phase 3a violations report as a separate deliverable. Do not auto-fix — hand off to code-tidy-sweep or address in a dedicated session.

Phase 4: Polish (parallel — sonnet workers)

Rule compliance sweep on the updated corpus. Launch 2–3 sonnet workers. Each worker loads:

  • docs/context/rules/docs.md only

Each worker scans its assigned pages for:

  • Filler phrases (“it should be noted”, “in order to”, “basically”)
  • Vague pronouns (“it”, “this”, “that” when the referent is not the immediately preceding noun)
  • Pages over 150 lines
  • Mixed topics on one page
  • Remaining cross-page duplication
  • Inconsistent terminology
  • Stale TODOs or placeholders
  • Prose that should be a table or link

Worker return format: punch list with exact current text and replacement text. Orchestrator executes edits.

Phase 5: Teardown (sequential)

docker stop lore-sweep-docs lore-sweep-source
docker rm lore-sweep-docs lore-sweep-source

Model Allocation

PhaseModelWorkersRule LoadedRationale
Phase 1 ReduceSonnet2–3docs.mdJudgment on what is duplicate or verbose
Phase 2a ExtractHaiku4docs.mdStructured extraction; fast and cheap
Phase 2b VerifySonnet4noneCode comprehension; no doc rules needed
Phase 3a Source ScanSonnet3 (1/repo)coding.mdOne worker per repo (lore, lore-docker, create-lore); commit-scoped
Phase 3b Doc CoverageSonnet3docs.mdUnderstands what a good doc entry covers
Fix SequenceOrchestrator1—Human-in-the-loop triage
Phase 4 PolishSonnet2–3docs.mdRule judgment; Haiku misses nuance

Rule isolation rule: workers get docs.md, coding.md, or nothing — never both. Doc workers get docs.md. Source-reading workers get coding.md (Phase 3a) or nothing (Phase 2b). No worker mixes rules.

Parallelization Diagram

Phase 0 (setup):     [docs-search :9190]   [source-search :9191]
                     ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Phase 1 (reduce):    [docs-A]  [docs-B]  [docs-C]
                     ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Phase 2a (extract):  [haiku-1] [haiku-2] [haiku-3] [haiku-4]
                     ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Phase 2b+3a (scan):  [verify-1][verify-2][verify-3][verify-4]  ← Phase 2b
                     [src-lore][src-docker][src-create]         ← Phase 3a (1/repo)
                     ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Phase 3b (coverage): [cov-1]   [cov-2]   [cov-3]
                     ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Fix sequence:        [Phase 2 fixes] → [Phase 3 additions] → [operator triage]
                     ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Phase 4 (polish):    [docs-A]  [docs-B]  [docs-C]
                     ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Phase 5 (teardown):  [stop + rm lore-sweep-docs lore-sweep-source]

Verification

After all phases:

wc -l docs/**/*.md   # All pages under 150 lines; exceptions need justification
git diff --stat       # Review total change magnitude before committing

Cadence

  • After major releases — hook, config, or platform behavior changes cause factual drift immediately
  • After doc-heavy sprints — new pages accumulate duplication and loose claims
  • Quarterly — general hygiene alongside code-tidy-sweep
  • Docs only (phases 1, 2, 4) — when source is stable but docs changed
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
